tEn: Deep reinforcement learning from human preferences
tZh: 基于人类偏好的深度强化学习
sEn: We can train complex RL behaviors using human preferences on trajectory segments, requiring feedback on less than 1% of agent interactions.
sZh: 我们可以通过人类对轨迹片段的偏好来训练复杂的强化学习行为，仅需对不到1%的智能体交互提供反馈。
absEn: For sophisticated reinforcement learning (RL) systems to interact usefully with real-world environments, we need to communicate complex goals to these systems. In this work, we explore goals defined in terms of (non-expert) human preferences between pairs of trajectory segments. We show that this approach can effectively solve complex RL tasks without access to the reward function, including Atari games and simulated robot locomotion, while providing feedback on less than one percent of our agent's interactions with the environment. This reduces the cost of human oversight far enough that it can be practically applied to state-of-the-art RL systems. To demonstrate the flexibility of our approach, we show that we can successfully train complex novel behaviors with about an hour of human time. These behaviors and environments are considerably more complex than any that have been previously learned from human feedback.
absZh: 为了让复杂的强化学习系统能够有效地与现实世界环境交互，我们需要向这些系统传达复杂的目标。在这项工作中，我们探索了基于（非专家）人类对轨迹片段对之间的偏好来定义目标。我们展示了这种方法可以在无需奖励函数的情况下有效解决复杂的强化学习任务，包括Atari游戏和模拟机器人运动，同时仅需对智能体与环境交互的不到1%提供反馈。这大大降低了人类监督的成本，使其能够实际应用于最先进的强化学习系统。为了展示我们方法的灵活性，我们证明可以用大约一小时的人类时间成功训练出复杂的新行为。这些行为和环境比以往任何通过人类反馈学习到的都要复杂得多。

contrib.en: Proposes a method to train deep RL agents from human preferences between trajectory segments, reducing human oversight cost.
contrib.zh: 提出一种从人类对轨迹片段的偏好中训练深度强化学习智能体的方法，降低了人类监督成本。

contrib.en: Demonstrates solving complex RL tasks (Atari games, robot locomotion) with less than 1% of agent-environment interactions labeled.
contrib.zh: 展示了在不到1%的智能体-环境交互被标注的情况下，解决复杂强化学习任务（Atari游戏、机器人运动）。

contrib.en: Shows novel behaviors (backflip, one-leg stand) can be learned from about an hour of human feedback.
contrib.zh: 展示了从约一小时的人类反馈中学习新颖行为（后空翻、单腿站立）。

contrib.en: Introduces an ensemble of reward predictors and online query selection to improve learning efficiency.
contrib.zh: 引入奖励预测器集成和在线查询选择以提高学习效率。

contrib.en: Provides evidence that human feedback can be economically scaled to state-of-the-art deep RL systems.
contrib.zh: 提供了人类反馈可经济地扩展到最先进深度强化学习系统的证据。

limits.en: Method may fail on tasks where short clips are confusing, e.g., Qbert with real human feedback.
limits.zh: 方法在短片段令人困惑的任务上可能失败，例如使用真实人类反馈的Qbert。

limits.en: Offline reward predictor training leads to poor performance due to nonstationary occupancy distribution.
limits.zh: 离线奖励预测器训练因占用分布的非平稳性导致性能不佳。

limits.en: Human feedback quality depends on rater consistency; errors or inconsistency can degrade performance.
limits.zh: 人类反馈质量取决于标注者一致性；错误或不一致会降低性能。

limits.en: Method assumes human preferences are generated by a reward function, which may not hold in all domains.
limits.zh: 方法假设人类偏好由奖励函数生成，这在所有领域可能不成立。

limits.en: Query selection based on ensemble variance is a crude approximation; better information-theoretic approaches remain future work.
limits.zh: 基于集成方差的查询选择是粗略近似；更好的信息论方法留待未来工作。

items.en: For sophisticated reinforcement learning (RL) systems to interact usefully with real-world environments, we need to communicate complex goals to these systems. In this work, we explore goals defined in terms of (non-expert) human preferences between pairs of trajectory segments. We show that this approach can effectively solve complex RL tasks without access to the reward function, including Atari games and simulated robot locomotion, while providing feedback on less than 1% of our agent’s interactions with the environment. This reduces the cost of human oversight far enough that it can be practically applied to state-of-the-art RL systems. To demonstrate the flexibility of our approach, we show that we can successfully train complex novel behaviors with about an hour of human time. These behaviors and environments are considerably more complex than any which have been previously learned from human feedback.
items.zh: 为了让复杂的强化学习（RL）系统能够有效地与现实世界环境交互，我们需要向这些系统传达复杂的目标。在这项工作中，我们探索了基于（非专家）人类对轨迹片段对之间偏好来定义的目标。我们表明，这种方法可以在无需奖励函数的情况下有效解决复杂的RL任务，包括Atari游戏和模拟机器人运动，同时仅需对智能体与环境交互的不到1%提供反馈。这将人类监督的成本降低到足以实际应用于最先进的RL系统。为了展示我们方法的灵活性，我们证明可以用大约一小时的人类时间成功训练出复杂的新行为。这些行为和环境比以往任何通过人类反馈学习到的都要复杂得多。

items.en: Recent success in scaling reinforcement learning (RL) to large problems has been driven in domains that have a well-specified reward function (Mnih et al., 2015, 2016; Silver et al., 2016). Unfortunately, many tasks involve goals that are complex, poorly-defined, or hard to specify. Overcoming this limitation would greatly expand the possible impact of deep RL and could increase the reach of machine learning more broadly.
items.zh: 最近，将强化学习（RL）扩展到大规模问题的成功主要发生在具有明确奖励函数的领域（Mnih 等人，2015，2016；Silver 等人，2016）。不幸的是，许多任务涉及的目标是复杂、定义不清或难以指定的。克服这一限制将极大地扩展深度强化学习的潜在影响，并可能更广泛地扩大机器学习的影响力。

items.en: For example, suppose that we wanted to use reinforcement learning to train a robot to clean a table or scramble an egg. It’s not clear how to construct a suitable reward function, which will need to be a function of the robot’s sensors. We could try to design a simple reward function that approximately captures the intended behavior, but this will often result in behavior that optimizes our reward function without actually satisfying our preferences. This difficulty underlies recent concerns about misalignment between our values and the objectives of our RL systems (Bostrom, 2014; Russell, 2016; Amodei et al., 2016). If we could successfully communicate our actual objectives to our agents, it would be a significant step towards addressing these concerns.
items.zh: 例如，假设我们想使用强化学习训练一个机器人来清理桌子或搅拌鸡蛋。目前尚不清楚如何构建一个合适的奖励函数，该函数需要依赖于机器人的传感器。我们可以尝试设计一个简单的奖励函数来近似捕捉预期行为，但这通常会导致行为优化了我们的奖励函数，却没有真正满足我们的偏好。这一困难是近期关于我们的价值观与强化学习系统目标之间错位问题的担忧的基础（Bostrom，2014；Russell，2016；Amodei 等人，2016）。如果我们能够成功地将我们的实际目标传达给智能体，那将是朝着解决这些担忧迈出的重要一步。

items.en: If we have demonstrations of the desired task, we can extract a reward function using inverse reinforcement learning (Ng and Russell, 2000). This reward function can then be used to train an agent with reinforcement learning. More directly, we can use imitation learning to clone the demonstrated behavior. However, these approaches are not directly applicable to behaviors that are difficult for humans to demonstrate (such as controlling a robot with many degrees of freedom but very non-human morphology).
items.zh: 如果我们有期望任务的演示，我们可以使用逆强化学习（Ng 和 Russell，2000）提取奖励函数。然后，该奖励函数可用于通过强化学习训练智能体。更直接地，我们可以使用模仿学习来克隆演示的行为。然而，这些方法不直接适用于人类难以演示的行为（例如，控制具有许多自由度但形态非常非人类的机器人）。

items.en: An alternative approach is to allow a human to provide feedback on our system’s current behavior and to use this feedback to define the task. In principle this fits within the paradigm of reinforcement learning, but using human feedback directly as a reward function is prohibitively expensive for RL systems that require hundreds or thousands of hours of experience. In order to practically train deep RL systems with human feedback, we need to decrease the amount of feedback required by several orders of magnitude.
items.zh: 另一种方法是允许人类对我们系统的当前行为提供反馈，并利用这些反馈来定义任务。原则上，这符合强化学习的范式，但直接将人类反馈用作奖励函数对于需要数百或数千小时经验的强化学习系统来说成本过高。为了实际地用人类反馈训练深度强化学习系统，我们需要将所需的反馈量减少几个数量级。

items.en: Our approach is to learn a reward function from human feedback and then to optimize that reward function. This basic approach has been considered previously, but we confront the challenges involved in scaling it up to modern deep RL and demonstrate by far the most complex behaviors yet learned from human feedback.
items.zh: 我们的方法是从人类反馈中学习一个奖励函数，然后优化该奖励函数。这种基本方法之前已被考虑过，但我们应对了将其扩展到现代深度强化学习所面临的挑战，并展示了迄今为止从人类反馈中学到的最复杂的行为。

items.en: In summary, we desire a solution to sequential decision problems without a well-specified reward function that
items.zh: 总之，我们期望在没有明确奖励函数的顺序决策问题中找到一个解决方案，该方案

items.en: enables us to solve tasks for which we can only recognize the desired behavior, but not necessarily demonstrate it,
items.zh: 使我们能够解决那些我们只能识别期望行为，但不一定能演示的任务，

items.en: allows agents to be taught by non-expert users,
items.zh: 允许非专家用户教导智能体，

items.capEn: Figure 1: Schematic illustration of our approach: the reward predictor is trained asynchronously from comparisons of trajectory segments, and the agent maximizes predicted reward.
items.capZh: 图1：我们方法的示意图：奖励预测器通过轨迹片段的比较进行异步训练，智能体最大化预测奖励。

items.en: Our algorithm fits a reward function to the human’s preferences while simultaneously training a policy to optimize the current predicted reward function (see Figure 1). We ask the human to compare short video clips of the agent’s behavior, rather than to supply an absolute numerical score. We found comparisons to be easier for humans to provide in some domains, while being equally useful for learning human preferences. Comparing short video clips is nearly as fast as comparing individual states, but we show that the resulting comparisons are significantly more helpful. Moreover, we show that collecting feedback online improves the system’s performance and prevents it from exploiting weaknesses of the learned reward function.
items.zh: 我们的算法同时拟合一个奖励函数以适应人类的偏好，并训练一个策略来优化当前预测的奖励函数（见图1）。我们要求人类比较智能体行为的短视频片段，而不是提供绝对的数值评分。我们发现，在某些领域，比较对人类来说更容易，同时对于学习人类偏好同样有用。比较短视频片段几乎与比较单个状态一样快，但我们表明，由此产生的比较显著更有帮助。此外，我们表明，在线收集反馈可以提高系统的性能，并防止它利用所学奖励函数的弱点。

items.en: Our experiments take place in two domains: Atari games in the Arcade Learning Environment (Bellemare et al., 2013), and robotics tasks in the physics simulator MuJoCo (Todorov et al., 2012). We show that a small amount of feedback from a non-expert human, ranging from fifteen minutes to five hours, suffices to learn most of the original RL tasks even when the reward function is not observable. We then consider some novel behaviors in each domain, such as performing a backflip or driving with the flow of traffic. We show that our algorithm can learn these behaviors from about an hour of feedback—even though it is unclear how to hand-engineer a reward function that would incentivize them.
items.zh: 我们的实验在两个领域进行：Arcade 学习环境中的 Atari 游戏（Bellemare 等人，2013）和物理模拟器 MuJoCo 中的机器人任务（Todorov 等人，2012）。我们表明，即使奖励函数不可观测，来自非专家人类的小量反馈（从十五分钟到五小时）也足以学习大多数原始强化学习任务。然后，我们考虑每个领域中的一些新颖行为，例如执行后空翻或随车流驾驶。我们表明，我们的算法可以从大约一小时的反馈中学习这些行为——尽管目前尚不清楚如何手工设计一个能够激励这些行为的奖励函数。

items.en: A long line of work studies reinforcement learning from human ratings or rankings, including Akrour et al. (2011), Pilarski et al. (2011), Akrour et al. (2012), Wilson et al. (2012), Sugiyama et al. (2012), Wirth and Fürnkranz (2013), Daniel et al. (2015), El Asri et al. (2016), Wang et al. (2016), and Wirth et al. (2016). Other lines of research considers the general problem of reinforcement learning from preferences rather than absolute reward values (Fürnkranz et al., 2012; Akrour et al., 2014), and optimizing using human preferences in settings other than reinforcement learning (Machwe and Parmee, 2006; Secretan et al., 2008; Brochu et al., 2010; Sørensen et al., 2016).
items.zh: 一系列长期的研究工作探讨了基于人类评分或排名的强化学习，包括 Akrour 等人 (2011)、Pilarski 等人 (2011)、Akrour 等人 (2012)、Wilson 等人 (2012)、Sugiyama 等人 (2012)、Wirth 和 Fürnkranz (2013)、Daniel 等人 (2015)、El Asri 等人 (2016)、Wang 等人 (2016) 以及 Wirth 等人 (2016)。其他研究方向考虑了基于偏好而非绝对奖励值的强化学习一般问题（Fürnkranz 等人，2012；Akrour 等人，2014），以及在强化学习之外的场景中使用人类偏好进行优化（Machwe 和 Parmee，2006；Secretan 等人，2008；Brochu 等人，2010；Sørensen 等人，2016）。

items.en: Our algorithm follows the same basic approach as Akrour et al. (2012) and Akrour et al. (2014). They consider continuous domains with four degrees of freedom and small discrete domains, where they can assume that the reward is linear in the expectations of hand-coded features. We instead consider physics tasks with dozens of degrees of freedom and Atari tasks with no hand-engineered features; the complexity of our environments force us to use different RL algorithms and reward models, and to cope with different algorithmic tradeoffs. One notable difference is that Akrour et al. (2012) and Akrour et al. (2014) elicit preferences over whole trajectories rather than short clips. So although we gather about two orders of magnitude more comparisons, our experiments require less than one order of magnitude more human time. Other differences focus on changing our training procedure to cope with the nonlinear reward models and modern deep RL, for example using asynchronous training and ensembling.
items.zh: 我们的算法遵循与 Akrour 等人 (2012) 和 Akrour 等人 (2014) 相同的基本方法。他们考虑具有四个自由度的连续域和小型离散域，并假设奖励是手工编码特征期望的线性函数。我们则考虑具有数十个自由度的物理任务和没有手工设计特征的 Atari 任务；我们环境的复杂性迫使我们使用不同的强化学习算法和奖励模型，并应对不同的算法权衡。一个显著的区别是，Akrour 等人 (2012) 和 Akrour 等人 (2014) 收集的是对整个轨迹的偏好，而不是短片段。因此，尽管我们收集的比较数量大约多两个数量级，但我们的实验所需的人类时间却少一个数量级。其他区别在于调整我们的训练过程以应对非线性奖励模型和现代深度强化学习，例如使用异步训练和集成方法。

items.en: Our approach to feedback elicitation closely follows Wilson et al. (2012). However, Wilson et al. (2012) assumes that the reward function is the distance to some unknown “target” policy (which is itself a linear function of hand-coded features). They fit this reward function using Bayesian inference, and rather than performing RL they produce trajectories using the MAP estimate of the target policy. Their experiments involve “synthetic” human feedback which is drawn from their Bayesian model, while we perform experiments with feedback gathered from non-expert users. It is not clear if the methods in Wilson et al. (2012) can be extended to complex tasks or if they can work with real human feedback.
items.zh: 我们的反馈收集方法紧密遵循 Wilson 等人 (2012)。然而，Wilson 等人 (2012) 假设奖励函数是到某个未知“目标”策略的距离（该策略本身是手工编码特征的线性函数）。他们使用贝叶斯推断拟合该奖励函数，并且不进行强化学习，而是使用目标策略的最大后验估计生成轨迹。他们的实验涉及从贝叶斯模型中抽取的“合成”人类反馈，而我们则使用从非专家用户收集的反馈进行实验。目前尚不清楚 Wilson 等人 (2012) 的方法是否可以扩展到复杂任务，或者是否能够处理真实的人类反馈。

items.en: MacGlashan et al. (2017), Pilarski et al. (2011), Knox and Stone (2009), and Knox (2012) perform experiments involving reinforcement learning from actual human feedback, although their algorithmic approach is less similar. In MacGlashan et al. (2017) and Pilarski et al. (2011), learning only occurs during episodes where the human trainer provides feedback. This appears to be infeasible in domains like Atari games where thousands of hours of experience are required to learn a high-quality policy, and would be prohibitively expensive even for the simplest tasks we consider. TAMER (Knox, 2012; Knox and Stone, 2013) also learn a reward function, however they consider much simpler settings where the desired policy can be learned relatively quickly.
items.zh: MacGlashan 等人 (2017)、Pilarski 等人 (2011)、Knox 和 Stone (2009) 以及 Knox (2012) 进行了涉及从真实人类反馈中进行强化学习的实验，尽管他们的算法方法不太相似。在 MacGlashan 等人 (2017) 和 Pilarski 等人 (2011) 中，学习仅发生在人类训练者提供反馈的回合中。这在 Atari 游戏等领域似乎不可行，因为学习高质量策略需要数千小时的体验，即使对于我们考虑的最简单任务，这也将过于昂贵。TAMER（Knox，2012；Knox 和 Stone，2013）也学习奖励函数，但他们考虑的是更简单的设置，其中所需策略可以相对快速地学习。

items.en: Our work could also be seen of a specific instance of the cooperative inverse reinforcement learning framework (Hadfield-Menell et al., 2016). This framework considers a two-player game between a human and a robot interacting with an environment with the purpose of maximizing the human’s reward function. In our setting the human is only allowed to interact with this game by stating their preferences.
items.zh: 我们的工作也可以被视为协作逆强化学习框架（Hadfield-Menell 等人，2016）的一个具体实例。该框架考虑人类和机器人与环境交互的两玩家游戏，目的是最大化人类的奖励函数。在我们的设置中，人类只能通过表达偏好来与这个游戏交互。

items.en: Compared to all prior work, our key contribution is to scale human feedback up to deep reinforcement learning and to learn much more complex behaviors. This fits into a recent trend of scaling reward learning methods to large deep learning systems, for example inverse RL (Finn et al., 2016), imitation learning (Ho and Ermon, 2016; Stadie et al., 2017), semi-supervised skill generalization (Finn et al., 2017), and bootstrapping RL from demonstrations (Silver et al., 2016; Hester et al., 2017).
items.zh: 与所有先前工作相比，我们的关键贡献是将人类反馈扩展到深度强化学习，并学习更复杂的行为。这符合最近将奖励学习方法扩展到大型深度学习系统的趋势，例如逆强化学习（Finn 等人，2016）、模仿学习（Ho 和 Ermon，2016；Stadie 等人，2017）、半监督技能泛化（Finn 等人，2017）以及从演示中引导强化学习（Silver 等人，2016；Hester 等人，2017）。

items.en: We consider an agent interacting with an environment over a sequence of steps; at each time t𝑡t the agent receives an observation ot∈𝒪subscript𝑜𝑡𝒪o_{t}\in\mathcal{O} from the environment and then sends an action at∈𝒜subscript𝑎𝑡𝒜a_{t}\in\mathcal{A} to the environment.
items.zh: 我们考虑一个智能体在多个时间步上与环境的交互过程；在每个时间步 t，智能体从环境中接收一个观测 o_t ∈ O，然后向环境发送一个动作 a_t ∈ A。

items.en: In traditional reinforcement learning, the environment would also supply a reward rt∈ℝsubscript𝑟𝑡ℝr_{t}\in\mathbb{R} and the agent’s goal would be to maximize the discounted sum of rewards. Instead of assuming that the environment produces a reward signal, we assume that there is a human overseer who can express preferences between trajectory segments. A trajectory segment is a sequence of observations and actions, σ=((o0,a0),(o1,a1),…,(ok−1,ak−1))∈(𝒪×𝒜)k𝜎subscript𝑜0subscript𝑎0subscript𝑜1subscript𝑎1…subscript𝑜𝑘1subscript𝑎𝑘1superscript𝒪𝒜𝑘\sigma=\left(\left(o_{0},a_{0}\right),\left(o_{1},a_{1}\right),\ldots,\left(o_{k-1},a_{k-1}\right)\right)\in\left(\mathcal{O}\times\mathcal{A}\right)^{k}. Write σ1≻σ2succeedssuperscript𝜎1superscript𝜎2\sigma^{1}\succ\sigma^{2} to indicate that the human preferred trajectory segment σ1superscript𝜎1\sigma^{1} to trajectory segment σ2superscript𝜎2\sigma^{2}. Informally, the goal of the agent is to produce trajectories which are preferred by the human, while making as few queries as possible to the human.
items.zh: 在传统的强化学习中，环境还会提供一个奖励 r_t ∈ R，智能体的目标是最大化折扣奖励之和。我们不假设环境产生奖励信号，而是假设存在一个人类监督者，他可以在轨迹片段之间表达偏好。轨迹片段是观测和动作的序列，σ = ((o_0, a_0), (o_1, a_1), …, (o_{k-1}, a_{k-1})) ∈ (O × A)^k。记 σ^1 ≻ σ^2 表示人类偏好轨迹片段 σ^1 胜过 σ^2。非正式地说，智能体的目标是产生人类偏好的轨迹，同时尽可能少地向人类提问。

items.en: More precisely, we will evaluate our algorithms’ behavior in two ways:
items.zh: 更精确地说，我们将从两个方面评估算法的行为：

items.en: We say that preferences ≻succeeds\succ are generated by a reward function111Here we assume here that the reward is a function of the observation and action. In our experiments in Atari environments, we instead assume the reward is a function of the preceding 4 observations. In a general partially observable environment, we could instead consider reward functions that depend on the whole sequence of observations, and model this reward function with a recurrent neural network. r:𝒪×𝒜→ℝ:𝑟→𝒪𝒜ℝr:\mathcal{O}\times\mathcal{A}\rightarrow\mathbb{R} if
items.zh: 如果偏好 ≻ 是由奖励函数 r: O × A → ℝ 生成的，则满足：

items.en: If the human’s preferences are generated by a reward function r𝑟r, then our agent ought to receive a high total reward according to r𝑟r. So if we know the reward function r𝑟r, we can evaluate the agent quantitatively. Ideally the agent will achieve reward nearly as high as if it had been using RL to optimize r𝑟r.
items.zh: 如果人类的偏好是由奖励函数 r 生成的，那么我们的智能体应当根据 r 获得较高的总奖励。因此，如果我们知道奖励函数 r，就可以定量评估智能体。理想情况下，智能体获得的奖励应接近它直接使用强化学习优化 r 所能获得的奖励。

items.en: Sometimes we have no reward function by which we can quantitatively evaluate behavior (this is the situation where our approach would be practically useful). In these cases, all we can do is qualitatively evaluate how well the agent satisfies to the human’s preferences. In this paper, we will start from a goal expressed in natural language, ask a human to evaluate the agent’s behavior based on how well it fulfills that goal, and then present videos of agents attempting to fulfill that goal.
items.zh: 有时我们没有可以定量评估行为的奖励函数（这正是我们的方法实际有用的情况）。在这些情况下，我们只能定性评估智能体满足人类偏好的程度。在本文中，我们将从自然语言表达的目标出发，请人类根据智能体实现该目标的程度来评估其行为，然后展示智能体尝试实现该目标的视频。

items.en: Our model based on trajectory segment comparisons is very similar to the trajectory preference queries used in Wilson et al. (2012), except that we don’t assume that we can reset the system to an arbitrary state222Wilson et al. (2012) also assumes the ability to sample reasonable initial states. But we work with high dimensional state spaces for which random states will not be reachable and the intended policy inhabits a low-dimensional manifold. and so our segments generally begin from different states. This complicates the interpretation of human comparisons, but we show that our algorithm overcomes this difficulty even when the human raters have no understanding of our algorithm.
items.zh: 我们基于轨迹片段比较的模型与 Wilson 等人（2012）中使用的轨迹偏好查询非常相似，区别在于我们不假设可以将系统重置到任意状态，因此我们的片段通常从不同的状态开始。这增加了人类比较的复杂性，但我们表明，即使人类评分者对我们的算法一无所知，我们的算法也能克服这一困难。

items.en: At each point in time our method maintains a policy π:𝒪→𝒜:𝜋→𝒪𝒜\pi:\mathcal{O}\rightarrow\mathcal{A} and a reward function estimate r^:𝒪×𝒜→ℝ:^𝑟→𝒪𝒜ℝ\hat{r}:\mathcal{O}\times\mathcal{A}\rightarrow\mathbb{R}, each parametrized by deep neural networks.
items.zh: 在每个时间点，我们的方法维护一个策略 π:𝒪→𝒜 和一个奖励函数估计 r̂:𝒪×𝒜→ℝ，两者均由深度神经网络参数化。

items.en: These networks are updated by three processes:
items.zh: 这些网络通过三个过程更新：

items.en: The policy π𝜋\pi interacts with the environment to produce a set of trajectories {τ1,…,τi}superscript𝜏1…superscript𝜏𝑖\{\tau^{1},\ldots,\tau^{i}\}. The parameters of π𝜋\pi are updated by a traditional reinforcement learning algorithm, in order to maximize the sum of the predicted rewards rt=r^​(ot,at)subscript𝑟𝑡^𝑟subscript𝑜𝑡subscript𝑎𝑡r_{t}=\hat{r}\left(o_{t},a_{t}\right).
items.zh: 策略 π 与环境交互，生成一组轨迹 {τ¹,…,τⁱ}。π 的参数通过传统的强化学习算法更新，以最大化预测奖励 r_t = r̂(o_t, a_t) 的总和。

items.en: We select pairs of segments (σ1,σ2)superscript𝜎1superscript𝜎2\left(\sigma^{1},\sigma^{2}\right) from the trajectories {τ1,…,τi}superscript𝜏1…superscript𝜏𝑖\{\tau^{1},\ldots,\tau^{i}\} produced in step 1, and send them to a human for comparison.
items.zh: 我们从步骤1生成的轨迹 {τ¹,…,τⁱ} 中选择成对的片段 (σ¹, σ²)，并将其发送给人类进行比较。

items.en: The parameters of the mapping r^^𝑟\hat{r} are optimized via supervised learning to fit the comparisons collected from the human so far.
items.zh: 通过监督学习优化 r̂ 的参数，以拟合迄今为止从人类收集的比较结果。

items.en: These processes run asynchronously, with trajectories flowing from process (1) to process (2), human comparisons flowing from process (2) to process (3), and parameters for r^^𝑟\hat{r} flowing from process (3) to process (1). The following subsections provide details on each of these processes.
items.zh: 这些过程异步运行，轨迹从过程(1)流向过程(2)，人类比较从过程(2)流向过程(3)，r̂ 的参数从过程(3)流向过程(1)。以下小节提供每个过程的详细信息。

items.en: After using r^^𝑟\hat{r} to compute rewards, we are left with a traditional reinforcement learning problem. We can solve this problem using any RL algorithm that is appropriate for the domain. One subtlety is that the reward function r^^𝑟\hat{r} may be non-stationary, which leads us to prefer methods which are robust to changes in the reward function. This led us to focus on policy gradient methods, which have been applied successfully for such problems (Ho and Ermon, 2016).
items.zh: 使用 r̂ 计算奖励后，我们面临一个传统的强化学习问题。我们可以使用任何适用于该领域的强化学习算法来解决这个问题。一个微妙之处在于奖励函数 r̂ 可能是非平稳的，这使我们倾向于选择对奖励函数变化鲁棒的方法。这促使我们专注于策略梯度方法，这些方法已成功应用于此类问题（Ho and Ermon, 2016）。

items.en: In this paper, we use advantage actor-critic (A2C; Mnih et al., 2016) to play Atari games, and trust region policy optimization (TRPO; Schulman et al., 2015) to perform simulated robotics tasks. In each case, we used parameter settings which have been found to work well for traditional RL tasks. The only hyperparameter which we adjusted was the entropy bonus for TRPO. This is because TRPO relies on the trust region to ensure adequate exploration, which can lead to inadequate exploration if the reward function is changing.
items.zh: 在本文中，我们使用优势演员-评论家（A2C; Mnih et al., 2016）来玩Atari游戏，并使用信任区域策略优化（TRPO; Schulman et al., 2015）来执行模拟机器人任务。在每种情况下，我们使用了在传统强化学习任务中表现良好的参数设置。唯一调整的超参数是TRPO的熵奖励。这是因为TRPO依赖信任区域来确保充分的探索，如果奖励函数发生变化，可能导致探索不足。

items.en: We normalized the rewards produced by r^^𝑟\hat{r} to have zero mean and constant standard deviation. This is a typical preprocessing step which is particularly appropriate here since the position of the rewards is underdetermined by our learning problem.
items.zh: 我们将 r̂ 产生的奖励归一化为零均值和恒定标准差。这是一个典型的预处理步骤，在这里特别合适，因为奖励的位置在我们的学习问题中是不确定的。

items.en: The human overseer is given a visualization of two trajectory segments, in the form of short movie clips. In all of our experiments, these clips are between 1 and 2 seconds long.
items.zh: 人类监督者会看到两个轨迹片段的可视化，以短视频片段的形式呈现。在我们所有的实验中，这些片段长度为1到2秒。

items.en: The human then indicates which segment they prefer, that the two segments are equally good, or that they are unable to compare the two segments.
items.zh: 然后，人类指示他们偏好哪个片段，两个片段同样好，或者无法比较这两个片段。

items.en: The human judgments are recorded in a database 𝒟𝒟\mathcal{D} of triples (σ1,σ2,μ)superscript𝜎1superscript𝜎2𝜇\left(\sigma^{1},\sigma^{2},\mu\right), where σ1superscript𝜎1\sigma^{1} and σ2superscript𝜎2\sigma^{2} are the two segments and μ𝜇\mu is a distribution over {1,2}12\{1,2\} indicating which segment the user preferred. If the human selects one segment as preferable, then μ𝜇\mu puts all of its mass on that choice. If the human marks the segments as equally preferable, then μ𝜇\mu is uniform. Finally, if the human marks the segments as incomparable, then the comparison is not included in the database.
items.zh: 人类判断记录在数据库 𝒟 中，包含三元组 (σ¹, σ², μ)，其中 σ¹ 和 σ² 是两个片段，μ 是 {1,2} 上的分布，表示用户偏好哪个片段。如果人类选择一个片段为更优，则 μ 将所有质量放在该选择上。如果人类标记两个片段同样好，则 μ 是均匀分布。最后，如果人类标记片段为不可比较，则该比较不包含在数据库中。

items.en: We can interpret a reward function estimate r^^𝑟\hat{r} as a preference-predictor if we view r^^𝑟\hat{r} as a latent factor explaining the human’s judgments and assume that the human’s probability of preferring a segment σisuperscript𝜎𝑖\sigma^{i} depends exponentially on the value of the latent reward summed over the length of the clip:333Equation 1 does not use discounting, which could be interpreted as modeling the human to be indifferent about when things happen in the trajectory segment. Using explicit discounting or inferring the human’s discount function would also be reasonable choices.
items.zh: 如果我们把 r̂ 视为解释人类判断的潜在因素，并假设人类偏好片段 σⁱ 的概率取决于该片段上潜在奖励总和的指数，那么我们可以将奖励函数估计 r̂ 解释为偏好预测器：³³³ 方程1未使用折扣，这可以解释为建模人类对轨迹片段中事件发生的时间不敏感。使用显式折扣或推断人类的折扣函数也是合理的选择。

items.en: We choose r^^𝑟\hat{r} to minimize the cross-entropy loss between these predictions and the actual human labels:
items.zh: 我们选择 r̂ 以最小化这些预测与实际人类标签之间的交叉熵损失：

items.en: This follows the Bradley-Terry model (Bradley and Terry, 1952) for estimating score functions from pairwise preferences, and is the specialization of the Luce-Shephard choice rule (Luce, 2005; Shepard, 1957) to preferences over trajectory segments. It can be understood as equating rewards with a preference ranking scale analogous to the famous Elo ranking system developed for chess (Elo, 1978). Just as the difference in Elo points of two chess players estimates the probability of one player defeating the other in a game of chess, the difference in predicted reward of two trajectory segments estimates the probability that one is chosen over the other by the human.
items.zh: 这遵循了Bradley-Terry模型（Bradley and Terry, 1952）用于从成对偏好中估计得分函数，并且是Luce-Shephard选择规则（Luce, 2005; Shepard, 1957）在轨迹片段偏好上的特化。可以理解为将奖励等同于偏好排名尺度，类似于国际象棋中著名的Elo排名系统（Elo, 1978）。正如两位棋手的Elo分数差估计了一方在比赛中击败另一方的概率，两个轨迹片段的预测奖励差估计了人类选择其中一个的概率。

items.en: Our actual algorithm incorporates a number of modifications to this basic approach, which early experiments discovered to be helpful and which are analyzed in Section 3.3:
items.zh: 我们的实际算法包含了对这种基本方法的若干修改，早期实验发现这些修改是有帮助的，并在第3.3节中进行了分析：

items.en: We fit an ensemble of predictors, each trained on |𝒟|𝒟\left|\mathcal{D}\right| triples sampled from 𝒟𝒟\mathcal{D} with replacement. The estimate r^^𝑟\hat{r} is defined by independently normalizing each of these predictors and then averaging the results.
items.zh: 我们拟合一个预测器集成，每个预测器在从 𝒟 中有放回采样的 |𝒟| 个三元组上训练。估计 r̂ 通过独立归一化每个预测器然后平均结果来定义。

items.en: A fraction of 1/e1𝑒1/e of the data is held out to be used as a validation set for each predictor. We use ℓ2subscriptℓ2\ell_{2} regularization and adjust the regularization coefficient to keep the validation loss between 1.11.11.1 and 1.51.51.5 times the training loss. In some domains we also apply dropout for regularization.
items.zh: 每个预测器保留 1/e 的数据作为验证集。我们使用 ℓ₂ 正则化，并调整正则化系数以保持验证损失在训练损失的1.1到1.5倍之间。在某些领域，我们还应用dropout进行正则化。

items.en: Rather than applying a softmax directly as described in Equation 1, we assume there is a 10% chance that the human responds uniformly at random. Conceptually this adjustment is needed because human raters have a constant probability of making an error, which doesn’t decay to 0 as the difference in reward difference becomes extreme.
items.zh: 我们不直接应用方程1中的softmax，而是假设人类有10%的概率均匀随机响应。概念上，这种调整是必要的，因为人类评分者有一个恒定的犯错概率，不会随着奖励差异变得极端而衰减到0。

items.en: We decide how to query preferences based on an approximation to the uncertainty in the reward function estimator, similar to Daniel et al. (2014): we sample a large number of pairs of trajectory segments of length k𝑘k, use each reward predictor in our ensemble to predict which segment will be preferred from each pair, and then select those trajectories for which the predictions have the highest variance across ensemble members. This is a crude approximation and the ablation experiments in Section 3 show that in some tasks it actually impairs performance. Ideally, we would want to query based on the expected value of information of the query (Akrour et al., 2012; Krueger et al., 2016), but we leave it to future work to explore this direction further.
items.zh: 我们基于奖励函数估计器不确定性的近似来决定如何查询偏好，类似于Daniel等人（2014）：我们采样大量长度为k的轨迹片段对，使用集成中的每个奖励预测器预测每对中哪个片段会被偏好，然后选择那些预测在集成成员间方差最高的轨迹。这是一个粗略的近似，第3节的消融实验表明，在某些任务中它实际上损害了性能。理想情况下，我们希望基于查询的信息期望值进行查询（Akrour et al., 2012; Krueger et al., 2016），但我们将其留待未来工作进一步探索。

items.en: We implemented our algorithm in TensorFlow (Abadi et al., 2016). We interface with MuJoCo (Todorov et al., 2012) and the Arcade Learning Environment (Bellemare et al., 2013) through the OpenAI Gym (Brockman et al., 2016).
items.zh: 我们在TensorFlow（Abadi等人，2016）中实现了算法。通过OpenAI Gym（Brockman等人，2016）与MuJoCo（Todorov等人，2012）和Arcade学习环境（Bellemare等人，2013）进行交互。

full.sec: 3.1 Reinforcement Learning Tasks with Unobserved Rewards
full.secZh: 3.1 具有未观测奖励的强化学习任务

items.en: In our first set of experiments, we attempt to solve a range of benchmark tasks for deep RL without observing the true reward. Instead, the agent learns about the goal of the task only by asking a human which of two trajectory segments is better. Our goal is to solve the task in a reasonable amount of time using as few queries as possible.
items.zh: 在我们的第一组实验中，我们尝试在不观测真实奖励的情况下解决一系列深度强化学习的基准任务。相反，智能体仅通过询问人类两个轨迹段中哪一个更好来了解任务的目标。我们的目标是使用尽可能少的查询在合理的时间内完成任务。

items.en: In our experiments, feedback is provided by contractors who are given a 1-2 sentence description of each task before being asked to compare several hundred to several thousand pairs of trajectory segments for that task (see Appendix B for the exact instructions given to contractors). Each trajectory segment is between 1 and 2 seconds long. Contractors responded to the average query in 3-5 seconds, and so the experiments involving real human feedback required between 30 minutes and 5 hours of human time.
items.zh: 在我们的实验中，反馈由承包商提供，他们首先获得每个任务的1-2句描述，然后被要求对该任务的几百到几千对轨迹段进行比较（承包商收到的确切指令见附录B）。每个轨迹段长度为1到2秒。承包商对平均查询的响应时间为3-5秒，因此涉及真实人类反馈的实验需要30分钟到5小时的人工时间。

items.en: For comparison, we also run experiments using a synthetic oracle whose preferences over trajectories exactly reflect reward in the underlying task. That is, when the agent queries for a comparison, instead of sending the query to a human, we immediately reply by indicating a preference for whichever trajectory segment actually receives a higher reward in the underlying task444In the case of Atari games with sparse rewards, it is relatively common for two clips to both have zero reward in which case the oracle outputs indifference. Because we considered clips rather than individual states, such ties never made up a large majority of our data. Moreover, ties still provide significant information to the reward predictor as long as they are not too common.. We also compare to the baseline of RL training using the real reward. Our aim here is not to outperform but rather to do nearly as well as RL without access to reward information and instead relying on much scarcer feedback. Nevertheless, note that feedback from real humans does have the potential to outperform RL (and as shown below it actually does so on some tasks), because the human feedback might provide a better-shaped reward.
items.zh: 为了比较，我们还使用一个合成预言机进行实验，该预言机对轨迹的偏好完全反映底层任务的奖励。也就是说，当智能体请求比较时，我们不是将查询发送给人类，而是立即回复，指示对实际获得更高奖励的轨迹段的偏好。我们还与使用真实奖励进行RL训练的基线进行了比较。我们的目标不是超越，而是在无法获取奖励信息、仅依赖更稀疏反馈的情况下，做到与RL几乎一样好。尽管如此，请注意来自真实人类的反馈有可能超越RL（如下所示，在某些任务上确实如此），因为人类反馈可能提供更好的奖励塑造。

items.en: We describe the details of our experiments in Appendix A, including model architectures, modifications to the environment, and the RL algorithms used to optimize the policy.
items.zh: 我们在附录A中描述了实验的细节，包括模型架构、对环境的修改以及用于优化策略的RL算法。

items.capEn: Figure 2: Results on MuJoCo simulated robotics as measured on the tasks’ true reward. We compare our method using real human feedback (purple), our method using synthetic feedback provided by an oracle (shades of blue), and reinforcement learning using the true reward function (orange). All curves are the average of 5 runs, except for the real human feedback, which is a single run, and each point is the average reward over five consecutive batches. For Reacher and Cheetah feedback was provided by an author due to time constraints. For all other tasks, feedback was provided by contractors unfamiliar with the environments and with our algorithm. The irregular progress on Hopper is due to one contractor deviating from the typical labeling schedule.
items.capZh: 图2：在MuJoCo模拟机器人任务上，基于任务真实奖励的测量结果。我们将使用真实人类反馈的方法（紫色）、使用由预言机提供的合成反馈的方法（蓝色渐变）以及使用真实奖励函数的强化学习（橙色）进行比较。所有曲线均为5次运行的平均值，但真实人类反馈为单次运行，每个点是连续五个批次的平均奖励。对于Reacher和Cheetah，由于时间限制，反馈由一位作者提供。对于其他所有任务，反馈由不熟悉环境和算法的承包商提供。Hopper上的不规则进展是由于一位承包商偏离了标准标注计划。

items.en: The first tasks we consider are eight simulated robotics tasks, implemented in MuJoCo (Todorov et al., 2012), and included in OpenAI Gym (Brockman et al., 2016). We made small modifications to these tasks in order to avoid encoding information about the task in the environment itself (the modifications are described in detail in Appendix A). The reward functions in these tasks are linear functions of distances, positions and velocities, and all are a quadratic function of the features. We included a simple cartpole task (“pendulum”) for comparison, since this is representative of the complexity of tasks studied in prior work.
items.zh: 我们考虑的第一个任务是八个模拟机器人任务，这些任务在MuJoCo中实现，并包含在OpenAI Gym中。我们对这些任务进行了小幅修改，以避免在环境本身中编码任务信息（修改细节见附录A）。这些任务中的奖励函数是距离、位置和速度的线性函数，并且都是特征的二次函数。我们包含了一个简单的cartpole任务（“pendulum”）用于比较，因为这代表了先前工作中研究的任务复杂度。

items.en: Surprisingly, by 1400 labels our algorithm performs slightly better than if it had simply been given the true reward, perhaps because the learned reward function is slightly better shaped—the reward learning procedure assigns positive rewards to all behaviors that are typically followed by high reward.
items.zh: 令人惊讶的是，在1400个标签后，我们的算法表现略优于直接给定真实奖励的情况，这可能是因为学习到的奖励函数稍微更好塑造——奖励学习过程将正奖励分配给所有通常随后会获得高奖励的行为。

items.en: Real human feedback is typically only slightly less effective than the synthetic feedback; depending on the task human feedback ranged from being half as efficient as ground truth feedback to being equally efficient. On the Ant task the human feedback significantly outperformed the synthetic feedback, apparently because we asked humans to prefer trajectories where the robot was “standing upright,” which proved to be useful reward shaping. (There was a similar bonus in the RL reward function to encourage the robot to remain upright, but the simple hand-crafted bonus was not as useful.)
items.zh: 真实人类反馈通常仅略低于合成反馈的效果；根据任务的不同，人类反馈的效率从真实反馈的一半到与真实反馈相当不等。在Ant任务上，人类反馈显著优于合成反馈，显然是因为我们要求人类偏好机器人“直立站立”的轨迹，这被证明是有用的奖励塑造。（RL奖励函数中也有类似的奖励来鼓励机器人保持直立，但简单的手工奖励不如这个有用。）

items.capEn: Figure 3: Results on Atari games as measured on the tasks’ true reward. We compare our method using real human feedback (purple), our method using synthetic feedback provided by an oracle (shades of blue), and reinforcement learning using the true reward function (orange). All curves are the average of 3 runs, except for the real human feedback which is a single run, and each point is the average reward over about 150,000 consecutive frames.
items.capZh: 图3：在Atari游戏上，基于任务真实奖励的测量结果。我们将使用真实人类反馈的方法（紫色）、使用由预言机提供的合成反馈的方法（蓝色渐变）以及使用真实奖励函数的强化学习（橙色）进行比较。所有曲线均为3次运行的平均值，但真实人类反馈为单次运行，每个点是连续约150,000帧的平均奖励。

items.en: The second set of tasks we consider is a set of seven Atari games in the Arcade Learning Environment (Bellemare et al., 2013), the same games presented in Mnih et al., 2013.
items.zh: 我们考虑的第二组任务是Arcade学习环境中的七个Atari游戏，与Mnih等人2013年提出的游戏相同。

items.en: On most of the games real human feedback performs similar to or slightly worse than synthetic feedback with the same number of labels, and often comparably to synthetic feedback that has 40% fewer labels. This may be due to human error in labeling, inconsistency between different contractors labeling the same run, or the uneven rate of labeling by contractors, which can cause labels to be overly concentrated in narrow parts of state space. The latter problems could potentially be addressed by future improvements to the pipeline for outsourcing labels. On Qbert, our method fails to learn to beat the first level with real human feedback; this may be because short clips in Qbert can be confusing and difficult to evaluate. Finally, Enduro is difficult for A3C to learn due to the difficulty of successfully passing other cars through random exploration, and is correspondingly difficult to learn with synthetic labels, but human labelers tend to reward any progress towards passing cars, essentially shaping the reward and thus outperforming A3C in this game (the results are comparable to those achieved with DQN).
items.zh: 在大多数游戏中，真实人类反馈的表现与相同标签数量的合成反馈相似或略差，并且通常与少40%标签的合成反馈相当。这可能是由于人类标注错误、不同承包商标注同一运行的不一致性，或者承包商标注速率不均匀，导致标签过度集中在状态空间的狭窄部分。后一个问题可能通过未来对标注外包流程的改进来解决。在Qbert上，我们的方法未能学会用真实人类反馈通过第一关；这可能是因为Qbert中的短片段令人困惑且难以评估。最后，Enduro对于A3C来说很难学习，因为通过随机探索成功超越其他车辆很困难，因此用合成标签学习也很困难，但人类标注者倾向于奖励任何超越车辆的进展，本质上塑造了奖励，从而在这个游戏中超越了A3C（结果与使用DQN获得的结果相当）。

items.capEn: Figure 4: Four frames from a single backflip. The agent is trained to perform a sequence of backflips, landing upright each time. The video is available at this link.
items.capZh: 图4：单个后空翻的四帧画面。智能体被训练执行一系列后空翻，每次平稳落地。视频可在此链接获取。

items.en: Experiments with traditional RL tasks help us understand whether our method is effective, but the ultimate purpose of human interaction is to solve tasks for which no reward function is available.
items.zh: 对传统强化学习任务的实验有助于我们理解方法是否有效，但人类交互的最终目的是解决那些没有奖励函数的任务。

items.en: Using the same parameters as in the previous experiments, we show that our algorithm can learn novel complex behaviors. We demonstrate:
items.zh: 使用与先前实验相同的参数，我们展示了算法能够学习新颖的复杂行为。我们演示了：

items.en: The Hopper robot performing a sequence of backflips (see Figure 4). This behavior was trained using 900 queries in less than an hour. The agent learns to consistently perform a backflip, land upright, and repeat.
items.zh: Hopper 机器人执行一系列后空翻（见图 4）。该行为在不到一小时内使用 900 次查询训练完成。智能体学会持续完成后空翻、直立落地并重复。

items.en: The Half-Cheetah robot moving forward while standing on one leg. This behavior was trained using 800 queries in under an hour.
items.zh: Half-Cheetah 机器人单腿站立向前移动。该行为在不到一小时内使用 800 次查询训练完成。

items.en: Keeping alongside other cars in Enduro. This was trained with roughly 1,300 queries and 4 million frames of interaction with the environment; the agent learns to stay almost exactly even with other moving cars for a substantial fraction of the episode, although it gets confused by changes in background.
items.zh: 在 Enduro 游戏中与其他车辆并排行驶。该行为使用大约 1300 次查询和 400 万帧环境交互训练完成；智能体学会在剧集的大部分时间内几乎与其他移动车辆保持并排，尽管它会被背景变化所迷惑。

items.en: Videos of these behaviors can be found at this link. These behaviors were trained using feedback from the authors.
items.zh: 这些行为的视频可在此链接找到。这些行为使用作者的反馈进行训练。

items.en: In order to better understand the performance of our algorithm, we consider a range of modifications:
items.zh: 为了更好地理解我们算法的性能，我们考虑了一系列修改：

items.en: We pick queries uniformly at random rather than prioritizing queries for which there is disagreement (random queries).
items.zh: 我们均匀随机地选择查询，而不是优先选择存在分歧的查询（随机查询）。

items.en: We train only one predictor rather than an ensemble (no ensemble). In this setting, we also choose queries at random, since there is no longer an ensemble that we could use to estimate disagreement.
items.zh: 我们只训练一个预测器，而不是集成（无集成）。在这种情况下，我们也随机选择查询，因为不再有可用于估计分歧的集成。

items.en: We train on queries only gathered at the beginning of training, rather than gathered throughout training (no online queries).
items.zh: 我们仅在训练开始时收集的查询上进行训练，而不是在整个训练过程中收集（无在线查询）。

items.en: We remove the ℓ2subscriptℓ2\ell_{2} regularization and use only dropout (no regularization).
items.zh: 我们移除 ℓ2 正则化，仅使用 dropout（无正则化）。

items.en: On the robotics tasks only, we use trajectory segments of length 1 (no segments).
items.zh: 仅在机器人任务上，我们使用长度为 1 的轨迹片段（无片段）。

items.en: Rather than fitting r^^𝑟\hat{r} using comparisons, we consider an oracle which provides the true total reward over a trajectory segment, and fit r^^𝑟\hat{r} to these total rewards using mean squared error (target).
items.zh: 我们不使用比较来拟合 r̂，而是考虑一个提供轨迹片段真实总奖励的预言机，并使用均方误差将 r̂ 拟合到这些总奖励上（目标）。

items.en: The results are presented in Figure 5 for MuJoCo and Figure 6 for Atari.
items.zh: 结果如图 5（MuJoCo）和图 6（Atari）所示。

items.capEn: Figure 5: Performance of our algorithm on MuJoCo tasks after removing various components, as described in Section Section 3.3. All graphs are averaged over 5 runs, using 700 synthetic labels each.
items.capZh: 图5：移除不同组件后，我们的算法在MuJoCo任务上的性能，如第3.3节所述。所有图表均为5次运行的平均值，每次使用700个合成标签。

items.capEn: Figure 6: Performance of our algorithm on Atari tasks after removing various components, as described in Section 3.3. All curves are an average of 3 runs using 5,500 synthetic labels (see minor exceptions in Section A.2).
items.capZh: 图6：移除不同组件后，我们的算法在Atari任务上的性能，如第3.3节所述。所有曲线均为3次运行的平均值，使用5,500个合成标签（第A.2节中有少量例外）。

items.en: Of particular interest is the poor performance of offline reward predictor training; here we find that due to the nonstationarity of the occupancy distribution, the predictor captures only part of the true reward, and maximizing this partial reward can lead to bizarre behavior that is undesirable as measured by the true reward (Amodei et al., 2016). For instance, on Pong offline training sometimes leads our agent to avoid losing points but not to score points; this can result in extremely long volleys that repeat the same sequence of events ad infinitum (videos at this link). This type of behavior demonstrates that in general human feedback needs to be intertwined with RL learning rather than provided statically.
items.zh: 特别值得注意的是离线奖励预测器训练的性能较差；我们发现，由于占用分布的非平稳性，预测器仅捕获了真实奖励的一部分，最大化这部分奖励可能导致奇怪的行为，这些行为从真实奖励的角度来看是不可取的（Amodei 等人，2016）。例如，在 Pong 上，离线训练有时会导致我们的智能体避免失分但不主动得分；这可能导致极长的回合，重复相同的事件序列直至无穷（视频见此链接）。这种行为表明，一般来说，人类反馈需要与强化学习交织在一起，而不是静态地提供。

items.en: Our main motivation for eliciting comparisons rather than absolute scores was that we found it much easier for humans to provide consistent comparisons than consistent absolute scores, especially on the continuous control tasks and on the qualitative tasks in Section 3.2; nevertheless it seems important to understand how using comparisons affects performance. For continuous control tasks we found that predicting comparisons worked much better than predicting scores. This is likely because the scale of rewards varies substantially and this complicates the regression problem, which is smoothed significantly when we only need to predict comparisons. In the Atari tasks we clipped rewards and effectively only predicted the sign, avoiding these difficulties (this is not a suitable solution for the continuous control tasks because the relative magnitude of the reward are important to learning). In these tasks comparisons and targets had significantly different performance, but neither consistently outperformed the other.
items.zh: 我们选择使用比较而非绝对分数的主要动机是，我们发现人类更容易提供一致的比较而非一致的绝对分数，尤其是在连续控制任务和 3.2 节的定性任务上；尽管如此，理解使用比较如何影响性能似乎很重要。对于连续控制任务，我们发现预测比较比预测分数效果好得多。这可能是因为奖励的尺度变化很大，这使回归问题复杂化，而当我们只需要预测比较时，问题被显著平滑。在 Atari 任务中，我们裁剪了奖励并实际上只预测了符号，避免了这些困难（这对于连续控制任务不是合适的解决方案，因为奖励的相对幅度对学习很重要）。在这些任务中，比较和目标具有显著不同的性能，但两者都没有持续优于对方。

items.en: We also observed large performance differences when using single frames rather than clips555We only ran these tests on continuous control tasks because our Atari reward model depends on a sequence of consecutive frames rather than a single frame, as described in Section A.2. In order to obtain the same results using single frames we would need to have collected significantly more comparisons. In general we discovered that asking humans to compare longer clips was significantly more helpful per clip, and significantly less helpful per frame. We found that for short clips it took human raters a while just to understand the situation, while for longer clips the evaluation time was a roughly linear function of the clip length. We tried to choose the shortest clip length for which the evaluation time was linear. In the Atari environments we also found that it was often easier to compare longer clips because they provide more context than single frames.
items.zh: 我们还观察到使用单帧而非片段时性能差异很大。我们仅在连续控制任务上运行这些测试，因为我们的 Atari 奖励模型依赖于连续帧序列而非单帧，如 A.2 节所述。为了使用单帧获得相同的结果，我们需要收集更多的比较。总的来说，我们发现要求人类比较更长的片段每个片段更有帮助，但每帧的帮助更小。我们发现对于短片段，人类评估者需要一些时间来理解情况，而对于较长的片段，评估时间大致是片段长度的线性函数。我们尝试选择评估时间为线性时的最短片段长度。在 Atari 环境中，我们还发现比较更长的片段通常更容易，因为它们比单帧提供更多上下文。

items.en: Agent-environment interactions are often radically cheaper than human interaction. We show that by learning a separate reward model using supervised learning, it is possible to reduce the interaction complexity by roughly 3 orders of magnitude. Not only does this show that we can meaningfully train deep RL agents from human preferences, but also that we are already hitting diminishing returns on further sample-complexity improvements because the cost of compute is already comparable to the cost of non-expert feedback.666For the Atari experiments we are using a virtual machine with 16 CPUs and one Nvidia K80 GPU which costs ~$700/month on GCE. Training takes about a day, so the compute cost is ~$25. Training with 5k labels corresponds roughly to 5 hours of human labour, at US minimum wage this totals ~$36.
items.zh: 智能体-环境交互通常比人类交互便宜得多。我们表明，通过使用监督学习学习一个独立的奖励模型，可以将交互复杂度降低大约三个数量级。这不仅表明我们可以根据人类偏好有意义地训练深度强化学习智能体，而且还表明我们在进一步的样本复杂度改进上已经遇到收益递减，因为算力成本已经与非专家反馈的成本相当。666对于Atari实验，我们使用一台配备16个CPU和一个Nvidia K80 GPU的虚拟机，在GCE上每月费用约为700美元。训练大约需要一天，因此算力成本约为25美元。使用5000个标签进行训练大约相当于5小时的人力劳动，按美国最低工资计算，总计约36美元。

items.en: Although there is a large literature on preference elicitation and reinforcement learning from unknown reward functions, we provide the first evidence that these techniques can be economically scaled up to state-of-the-art reinforcement learning systems. This represents a step towards practical applications of deep RL to complex real-world tasks.
items.zh: 尽管关于偏好获取和从未知奖励函数进行强化学习已有大量文献，但我们首次提供了证据表明这些技术可以在经济上扩展到最先进的强化学习系统。这代表了向深度强化学习在复杂现实世界任务中实际应用迈出的一步。

items.en: Future work may be able to improve the efficiency of learning from human preferences, and expand the range of tasks to which it can be applied.
items.zh: 未来的工作可能能够提高从人类偏好中学习的效率，并扩展其可应用的任务范围。

items.en: In the long run it would be desirable to make learning a task from human preferences no more difficult than learning it from a programmatic reward signal, ensuring that powerful RL systems can be applied in the service of complex human values rather than low-complexity goals.
items.zh: 从长远来看，理想的情况是使从人类偏好中学习任务变得与从程序化奖励信号中学习一样容易，确保强大的强化学习系统能够服务于复杂的人类价值观，而不是低复杂度的目标。

items.en: We thank Olivier Pietquin, Bilal Piot, Laurent Orseau, Pedro Ortega, Victoria Krakovna, Owain Evans, Andrej Karpathy, Igor Mordatch, and Jack Clark for reading drafts of the paper. We thank Tyler Adkisson, Mandy Beri, Jessica Richards, Heather Tran, and other contractors for providing the data used to train our agents. Finally, we thank OpenAI and DeepMind for providing a supportive research environment and for supporting and encouraging this collaboration.
items.zh: 我们感谢Olivier Pietquin、Bilal Piot、Laurent Orseau、Pedro Ortega、Victoria Krakovna、Owain Evans、Andrej Karpathy、Igor Mordatch和Jack Clark阅读了论文的草稿。我们感谢Tyler Adkisson、Mandy Beri、Jessica Richards、Heather Tran以及其他承包商提供了用于训练我们智能体的数据。最后，我们感谢OpenAI和DeepMind提供了支持性的研究环境，并支持和鼓励了这次合作。
